introSection: Boomerang (Kyrgyz: Бумеранг) is a steel winged 4th dimension shuttle roller coaster located at Sky Park Baytik in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan. It was potentially the first roller coaster built by Pax Company since 2013.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In December 2024, the coaster was nearly completed.

Testing with riders began in March 2025, before the ride had undergone its safety inspection, which led the park to get a 20,000 UZS fine in April.

Boomerang opened with SkyPark Baytik on 1 June 2025.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
Boomerang is the first and currently only installation of Pax's Boomerang product, specifically the Two Loops model, a 4th dimension coaster with free spinning seats. It is tall,  long, and weighs approximately 117 tons.

The coaster's layout consists of a catch car lift hill, followed by an element similar to a saxophone, and a Vertical rollback, after which the layout is done in reverse.

fullText:
Boomerang (Kyrgyz: Бумеранг) is a steel winged 4th dimension shuttle roller coaster located at Sky Park Baytik in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan. It was potentially the first roller coaster built by Pax Company since 2013.

In December 2024, the coaster was nearly completed.

Testing with riders began in March 2025, before the ride had undergone its safety inspection, which led the park to get a 20,000 UZS fine in April.

Boomerang opened with SkyPark Baytik on 1 June 2025.

Boomerang is the first and currently only installation of Pax's Boomerang product, specifically the Two Loops model, a 4th dimension coaster with free spinning seats. It is tall,  long, and weighs approximately 117 tons.

The coaster's layout consists of a catch car lift hill, followed by an element similar to a saxophone, and a Vertical rollback, after which the layout is done in reverse.
